title: Measuring non-adherence
paperName: Paper 3 - Specialist Options: Approaches, Issues and Debates
summary: 9990 Health — self-report, pill counts, biochemical markers, and adherence measurement challenges.
learningObjectives: Outline methods: self-report, observation, biochemical tests.
Compare direct vs indirect measurement approaches.
Evaluate validity and reliability of each method.
Assess ethical issues in monitoring adherence.

simpleExplanation.title: How do we know they took it?
simpleExplanation.summary: Measuring adherence is tricky — patients overreport compliance, pill counts can be faked, and blood tests are costly but more objective.
steps: Self-report — cheap but socially desirable answers.
Pill count — objective but patients may discard pills.
Biochemical — detect drug metabolites in blood/urine.
Electronic monitors — MEMS caps track openings.

sections.content: **Measuring adherence** (3.2.2) — compare **methods** on validity, cost, and practicality.
